methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Anduril Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (SPAC), post-money $78B+

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a defense and security company at SPAC stage, is being valued against a peer set of 15 same-sector companies with similar valuation profiles. The comparable universe includes 8 private companies and 2 public equities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), with top private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Peer-median analysis forms the primary valuation method. The median valuation across the 15-company peer set is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. Representative comparables include Skydio at $2.2B (Series E), Chaos Industries at $4.5B (Series D), Shield AI at $5.6B (Series G), Flock Safety at $7.5B (Series E), and Saronic at $9.2B (Series D). This method's applicability is strong given stage and sector alignment within the peer cohort.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B–$1.7B, centered at the $1.5B peer median. True Anomaly's current $2.2B valuation sits 47% above the median, positioning it at the higher end of observed peer valuations for this stage. Key uncertainties include limited methodological triangulation (single method applied) and heterogeneity in peer funding stages, which span Series D through Series G, potentially masking stage-specific premium or discount dynamics relative to SPAC positioning.
